target.name: AlixLabs
target.founder: Jonas Sundqvist, Dmitry Suyatin, Amin Karimi

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
AlixLabs is a Series A semiconductor company founded by Jonas Sundqvist, Dmitry Suyatin, and Amin Karimi, having raised €15M to date. The valuation assessment draws on a peer set of 4 private companies operating in Chips & Semiconductors at Series A and adjacent stages: Substrate ($1.0B), Rain AI ($500M), Etched ($5.0B), and Positron AI ($1.0B).

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach given available data. The four same-sector, same-stage comparables yield a median valuation of $1.0B, with a range spanning $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied because AlixLabs' revenue is not publicly disclosed—a standard constraint for private company analysis. The three public semiconductor tickers (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) trade at a sector median P/S of 20.3x, but this multiple cannot be meaningfully applied without target revenue.

Based on peer-median triangulation alone, the analysis supports a valuation range of $850M–$1.1B, centered at $1.0B. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion within the peer set (500M to 5.0B), the absence of revenue data to calibrate public comparables, and the relative maturity of AlixLabs relative to each peer. Further diligence on technology differentiation and capital efficiency metrics would narrow this range.
